summary: First iteration testing of Amazon Ads Analytics app. Backend: 17/17 pytest cases passed (100%). Frontend: complete UI flow verified (upload → dashboard → campaigns → search-terms → history → theme/marketplace). AI recommendations endpoint with Claude Sonnet 4.5 via Emergent LLM key works end-to-end. No blocking bugs found.

minor.issue: detect_report_type() does not recognise Amazon 'Targeting' / 'Product Targeting' reports (has 'targeting' column but no customer_search_term/placement/campaign alias match), so report_type falls back to 'unknown' → UI shows 'Desconocido'. Consider adding a 'targeting' report_type detection branch.
No max file size validation: FastAPI will accept and load the entire file in memory (await file.read()) — risk of OOM with very large reports. Consider streaming or size cap.
Summary sent to LLM includes top 10 campaigns + 15 terms as a raw Python dict str(). Works, but serialising with json.dumps would be cleaner and slightly smaller tokens.

issues: When active dataset report_type is 'unknown', header pill shows 'Desconocido · SP' — label is fine but could be mapped to something like 'Targeting' when Keyword Text / Targeting is present but no search term column.

action_items: (Optional) Extend detect_report_type in /app/backend/amazon_ads.py to detect 'targeting' reports when 'keyword text'/'targeting'/'product targeting expression' headers exist but no search-term/placement column.
(Optional) Add upload size guard to POST /api/imports/upload.
(Optional) Serialise AI prompt payload with json.dumps for cleanliness.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py stores full parsed rows inside the dataset document (doc['rows']). For large Amazon reports this can exceed the 16MB MongoDB document limit. Consider storing rows in a separate collection keyed by dataset_id once row_count grows.
server.py:186 — ai_recommendations loads full doc including rows from Mongo even though only kpis, campaign/term aggregates are needed. Project only needed fields to reduce payload.

amazon_ads._to_number handles European decimals but the 'percent' path still divides-by-100 is NOT applied — values like '31.01%' are stored as 31.01 (which compute_kpis then re-derives, OK). Works, but worth a comment.

amazon_ads.detect_report_type 'campaign' detection runs any(a in h for h in norm) where h is the whole header string — this can falsely match short aliases; currently masked because search_term takes priority.
CORS_ORIGINS from env is split by comma but middleware is added AFTER include_router — order is fine in FastAPI but the current order means CORS headers are applied globally; acceptable.
No request/response logging middleware — hard to debug production issues.
Frontend ImportZone: no file-size / extension UI validation before POST (server returns 400, but UX could be improved).
seed_data_creation: Created and cleaned up TEST_EN_SearchTerm / TEST_ES_SearchTerm / TEST_XLSX / TEST_DELETE datasets during backend tests. One UI-created dataset (UI_TEST_EN) was deleted via the UI at the end of the Playwright run.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Backend tests live at /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py and cover the full public API surface (health, upload EN/ES/XLSX, invalid file, list/get/delete datasets, campaigns, search-terms with suggest_negative, timeseries date + campaign fallback, AI recs). Run: pytest /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Frontend data-testid coverage is complete: sidebar nav-*, header (marketplace-selector, theme-toggle, mp-{id}), import-zone, dropzone, file-input, dataset-name-input, pick-file-btn, view-dashboard, campaigns-table, campaign-filter, col-{key}, campaign-row-{i}, search-terms-table, term-filter, toggle-only-negatives, term-row-{i}, neg-badge-{i}, history-panel, history-item-{id}, activate-{id}, delete-{id}, empty-state. App has no auth. Known minor limitation: SP Targeting reports report_type maps to 'unknown'.
